India to tap Amazon, Flipkart data directly in inflation revamp, roll out services index NEW DELHI (Reuters) -India will start sourcing price data directly from e-commerce giants including Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart to overhaul its benchmark inflation gauge, aiming to capture shifting consumption habits and address concerns that current data is outdated, the head of the statistics ministry said.  The move could make Indias retail inflation data more robust by accounting for prices on online platforms as their share in household spending rises.  India had about 270 million online shoppers in 2024, a figure projected to grow 22% annually, a private study showed.
